raw_comment: This graph illustrates the time periods in which regions of our country saw their "first leaf," an indication that spring has sprung! The first thing I noticed was that the more south a state is on a map, the earlier it sees its first leaf. This makes sense as the more south a state is, the closer it is to the ecuator, making it warmer. I do wonder however why certain regions in the midwest experience spring later than others. It's a very disorganized pattern, too. The creators of this graph were very creative in terms of the animation they added as it shows the progression of states that experienced spring. A good title for this graph could be "I can't beLEAF spring is here!"
ace_sentences: Regions of our country saw their first leaf in these time periods.
A first leaf indicates that spring has sprung.
If a state is more south on a map, then the state sees its first leaf earlier.
If a state is more south, then the state is closer to the ecuator.
If a state is closer to the ecuator, then the state is warmer.
I wonder why certain regions in the midwest experience spring later than other regions.
The creators of this graph were creative.
The creators added animation to this graph.
The animation shows the progression of states that experienced spring.
A good title for this graph could be "I can't beLEAF spring is here!".
